sanskrit:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च।
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी॥
transliteration: yayā svapnaṃ bhayaṃ śokaṃ viṣādaṃ madam eva ca | na vimuñcati durmedhā dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī ||
literal_meaning: That by which (one) does not give up sleep, fear, grief, despondency and arrogance—such unintelligent resolve, O Pārtha, is tāmasī.
contextual_meaning: A rigid, low-energy mindset that clings to escapist fantasy, dread, self-pity, and grandiosity; it masquerades as ‘determination’ but is merely psychic inertia.
psychological_state: Learned helplessness fused with narcissistic defences; the ego fortifies itself against change by rehearsing catastrophic narratives and indulgent reveries.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling doom-feeds till 3 a.m., vowing to ‘start fresh tomorrow’ yet hitting snooze ten times, then calling in sick while binge-watching apocalyptic series.
anchor_text: Stubborn clinging to fear and fantasy is not resolve; it is inertia.
